body:
Dad,

Called you back from Sweetleaf — you'd already picked up Lily so I got Mateo's voicemail by accident. Peak efficiency from everyone involved.

Anyway. The 6:45 AM FaceTime is a hard no going forward. I had a shoot at Dobbin St. by 8 and I needed that last window. The light in that space is a 40-minute situation and you cut it close. Natural light doesn't negotiate.

The thing you flagged about the website — I looked at it. You're right, the portfolio section loads like it's 2009 broadband. I'll rework the landing page after the Olmsted editorial wraps next week. Appreciate the heads up even if the delivery method was aggressive.

— J

Jesse,

Noted on the 6:45. I keep forgetting you don't keep normal hours. Your mother does the same thing to Kevin — calls at 7 AM like it's a reasonable choice. At least I had a reason.

I wasn't criticizing the website, just noticed it on the iPad and the images took a while to load. What loaded looked great, though. Priya showed Lily the rooftop ones from that Brooklyn shoot and now apparently she wants to be a food photographer. You've been warned.

Let me know when the Olmsted piece runs. We'll look for it. And tell Mateo we said hello.

Love,
Dad

Hi,

Following up on my note from Thursday — the Bedford Ave location has been using a different oat milk brand for at least two weeks now. Barli was on the cup until last month; whatever replaced it doesn't steam the same way. The microfoam is breaking before I'm halfway down the block, and the mouthfeel is noticeably thinner. Not catastrophic, but I'm in there most mornings before shoots and it's become a small recurring thing.

Can you confirm whether the switch is permanent or a supply gap? Either way, good to know so I can calibrate accordingly.

— Jesse Page

Hi Jesse,

Thank you for writing in — and for the specificity, it's actually helpful. You're correct: about three weeks ago we transitioned from Barli to Minor Figures across our Brooklyn locations following a distributor disruption. The change wasn't communicated to regulars, and I apologize for that gap.

Minor Figures behaves differently under steam — higher water content, which is why the foam structure breaks down faster. Our baristas have been adjusting temperature and pour technique to compensate, but the results are still inconsistent. We're working toward a more stable approach by next week.

If it would help, I can flag your preference with the Bedford Ave team directly.

Howard
Sweetleaf Customer Experience

Howard,

Appreciate the breakdown — Minor Figures tracks. I've worked with it on set and it does exactly what you're describing under heat. The technique adjustment might help at the margins but the protein content is lower, so there's a ceiling on what steaming can fix.

No need to flag my account. I'll just ask them to pour faster, which I should probably be doing anyway given my schedule. Good to know it's not a permanent call — I'll check back in a few weeks.

— Jesse

Hi,

Second time this week my scheduled follow-up sequences haven't gone out. I run a morning communication block — Bedford Avenue, oat milk, inbox — and the whole point is that these queued emails move while I'm in that window. Today's batch didn't send: the Olmsted shoot follow-up, a proposal to Lula Mae, and a seasonal inquiry reply to Islas. Drafts are saved, segments look correct, nothing in the error log.

On the Studio plan. Account tied to jesse.page@jessepagephotography.com.

I have a shoot this afternoon. Need this resolved before I walk out the door.

Jesse

Hi Jesse,

Thanks for writing in. I can see the account and the held sends. The issue is your custom domain's DKIM record — it lapsed on jessepagephotography.com, so our system held the sequences rather than deliver them unsigned. Nothing was lost; the sends are queued, not failed.

To fix it: log into your DNS provider and re-add the DKIM record. I'm attaching the exact values. Propagation usually takes under an hour. Once it confirms on our end, I can release today's batch immediately rather than waiting for the next scheduled window.

Let me know when you've updated the record and I'll watch for propagation.

Howard
Customer Support

Howard,

Updated the DKIM record through Namecheap about forty minutes ago. I'm mid-edit right now — tethered to Lightroom, Tuesday's shoot, roughly 200 frames to cull — so I'm not going to be watching a propagation checker.

Release the three holds I named once it confirms on your end: Olmsted follow-up, Lula Mae proposal, Islas seasonal inquiry. Anything else in the queue past those, leave it held. I want to review before more goes out.

For the record: the morning communication block exists specifically so this kind of thing doesn't bleed into shoot days. It is bleeding into a shoot day.

Jesse

Hi Jesse,

Propagation confirmed at 2:14 PM. I've released the three sends — Olmsted follow-up, Lula Mae proposal, Islas seasonal inquiry. Delivery confirmations are already showing in your account logs. The remaining four queued items are still on hold per your instruction.

I've also added a domain authentication alert to your account set to flag 30 days before the DKIM record expires — should give you enough runway to renew without it landing on a shoot day.

Apologize for the disruption. Everything looks clean from here.

Howard
Customer Support

Hi Dad,

Quick update on the Greenpoint scout from Tuesday — finally nailed the rooftop location for the Botanica campaign. North-facing, raw concrete parapet, gets this diffused afternoon bounce off the water tower that's genuinely hard to manufacture in studio. I'm locking it in before the restaurant below changes their minds about access, which, given that they're on their third ownership group since March, is a real possibility.

Did a prop run Wednesday after my morning coffee at Sweetleaf — picked up two matte black ceramic plates from the estate shop on N 7th, the kind with the imperfect rim that reads as intentional rather than cheap. Paired with the linen I sourced in February, the overhead shots should have enough texture contrast to actually earn the rate they're paying.

Editing the dumpling series tonight. Four hundred frames from a two-hour shoot. The math on freelance is always funny until you're in hour three of culling.

Just wanted to loop you in since you asked last week. Call Sunday?

Jesse

Hi Jesse,

Thanks for the update — sounds like Tuesday paid off. A north-facing rooftop with natural bounce light is exactly the kind of thing you can't fake, so smart move locking it down quickly. Third ownership group since March is a detail that would make anyone move fast.

The prop sourcing sounds right. I've seen enough of your finished work to know that rim texture on ceramics is never a small decision with you — it ends up mattering in the final frame even when nobody can say exactly why.

Four hundred frames for a two-hour shoot. I've heard this math before. You'll get through it.

Sunday works well. Your mother will want to be on the call too, fair warning. She's been asking about the Botanica project since you mentioned it at dinner.

Talk soon,
Dad

Hi,

Still getting the 401 unauthorized error when trying to pull client emails through the API. Three days now. I've got a shoot Tuesday and my client communication block Monday morning is completely dead without access — that's two hours of billing follow-ups and delivery confirmations that aren't moving.

OAuth credentials are current, refreshed them Friday. Scopes are gmail.readonly and gmail.send. Token refresh isn't firing when the access token expires. This feels server-side, not my config.

Can you check whether project permissions were updated after the last ticket?

— Jesse

Hi Jesse,

Thanks for following up. I pulled the original ticket — the OAuth consent screen on your project got flagged for re-verification after a policy update we pushed last week. That's what's blocking the token refresh cycle.

I've submitted the re-verification request manually on our end. Access should restore within 24–48 hours. If you need something moving sooner, you can generate a short-lived authorization code through the OAuth Playground to cover the gap.

If access isn't back by end of day Tuesday, reply here and I'll escalate to Tier 2.

Howard
Gmail API Support

Hi Howard,

Access came back this morning. Token refresh is clean, pull is working. Good timing — shoot wrapped around 4 and I had selects due to a client by 6.

One thing worth flagging: the re-verification flow sent a security alert to my client-facing Google account. Two separate clients forwarded it asking if I'd been compromised. If that notification is standard, a heads-up in the confirmation email would prevent a small but unnecessary panic on the client side.

Otherwise — resolved. Appreciate the quick turnaround.

— Jesse

Hi Howard,

Following up on my earlier request — the Google Calendar API integration still isn't pulling my shoot blocks into the third-party scheduler. I've got three restaurant clients booked through July and I'm manually cross-referencing everything, which is eating into editing time I don't have.

The OAuth token refresh was supposed to be fixed in the last patch. It's not. Calendar events created directly in Google show up fine; anything pushed via the API from my booking form disappears. Reproducible every time.

Can you confirm whether the fix actually shipped or if it's still queued? Happy to share console logs if that moves things faster.

Jesse Page
Jesse Page Photography, Brooklyn

Hi Jesse,

Thank you for following up and sorry for the friction this is causing your workflow.

I checked internally — the OAuth token refresh patch did ship on the 10th, but we identified a secondary issue late last week: write permissions for calendar event creation via the API are dropping when the token refreshes if the original authorization scope didn't include `calendar.events` explicitly. That's likely what you're hitting.

To fix it on your end without waiting for another patch: revoke the existing OAuth token in your Google account settings, then reauthorize through the booking form so it requests the full scope fresh. It should take about two minutes. If events still don't appear after reauthorizing, reply back and I'll escalate to the API team directly.

Let me know how it goes.

Best,
Howard
Platform Support

Howard,

Re-auth worked — events are populating now. Tested with a this shoot block and it pushed through cleanly.

One thing worth flagging: the scope gap isn't documented anywhere in the setup flow. I only caught the original issue because I noticed my July Brooklyn loft shoot wasn't syncing with my assistant's calendar. Someone less detail-oriented would've missed it for weeks.

Worth a note in your API docs or at minimum a warning during the auth step. Freelancers running solo operations don't always have a fallback when booking logistics break.

Thanks for the fast turnaround on this one.

Jesse

Jesse,

Glad the reauth resolved it — and that's a fair point about the documentation gap. I've filed an internal request to add a callout in the OAuth setup guide specifically flagging the `calendar.events` scope requirement. The auth flow UI is also on the roadmap for a permissions summary screen, which should surface this before it becomes a problem.

I'm marking the ticket resolved on our end, but I'll keep it linked to the doc update so there's a paper trail. If anything else surfaces — especially around the API and scheduling — feel free to reference this request in a new ticket and it'll route back to me.

Appreciate the specifics you provided. Made it a lot faster to diagnose.

Best,
Howard
Platform Support

Hi Howard,

Following up on my earlier request from last week — still seeing the same permissions error when the app tries to pull reach data via the Basic Display API. The error reads: "OAuthException: (#10) This endpoint requires the 'pages_read_engagement' permission." I've re-authorized three times.

For context, I use this integration to pull post performance for client recap decks. Right now I'm rebuilding the numbers manually from the native app, which is fine short-term but not sustainable when I'm delivering reports for four restaurant clients simultaneously.

Can you confirm whether the permission scope actually got updated on your end after last Tuesday's call, or if I need to resubmit the app review? The review page still shows "In Progress" with no timestamp.

Thanks,
Jesse Page
Jesse Page Photography

Hi Jesse,

Apologies for the delay — I tracked this down with our integrations team this morning. The permission scope update was queued but did not propagate correctly after Tuesday's session. That's on us.

I've manually pushed the corrected scope to your app ID (2284710938). You'll need to deauthorize and reauthorize the connected Instagram Business Account once more — this should be the last time. After that, the 'pages_read_engagement' and 'instagram_manage_insights' permissions will both be active.

If you're still hitting the OAuthException after reauthorizing, reply here with a fresh error log and I'll escalate directly to Tier 2 rather than routing through standard review.

Again, sorry for the friction. You should be good to go within the hour.

Best,
Howard Page
Developer Support | Meta for Developers

Hi Howard,

Following up on my earlier request from last Tuesday. The webhook endpoint is still returning 400s on invoice.payment_succeeded events — happens consistently when clients pay via ACH, not card. I've got six outstanding invoices sitting in limbo right now, which is not a great position for a freelancer to be in at mid-month.

I've already checked the endpoint secret, rotated it twice, confirmed the URL is live. The Stripe logs show the event firing fine on your end; something's dropping it between there and my site. Screenshots attached.

Is there a known issue with ACH webhooks on the current API version? Running 2023-10-16.

— Jesse
Jesse Page Photography

Hi Jesse,

Thanks for the follow-up and sorry for the delay on this — I've pulled up your earlier request.

Looking at the logs on our end, the 400s are consistent with a signature verification mismatch. This sometimes happens after a secret rotation if the old secret is still cached in an environment variable somewhere. The fix is usually confirming that every environment — local, staging, production — is reading the new secret from the same source.

One other thing to check: if your server is behind a proxy or load balancer, the raw request body can get re-encoded before it reaches your verification function, which breaks the signature. Stripe requires the raw, unparsed body for webhook validation.

Let me know if either of those surfaces anything. Happy to jump on a call if it's faster.

Best,
Howard Page
Stripe Developer Support

Howard,

The proxy was it. Nginx was parsing the body before it hit the handler — classic. Added the raw body middleware, tested against a few live events, all green now. The six invoices reconciled correctly too.

Appreciate you narrowing it down. Would've been staring at the secret rotation for another two days if you hadn't flagged the proxy angle — and I had a shoot in Williamsburg this morning that was already eating into my troubleshooting window.

You can close the ticket.

— Jesse
Jesse Page Photography
